017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart;
018
019import static java.util.Collections.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.HttpPartDataType.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.HttpPartFormat.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.list;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ObjectUtils.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
028import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.eq;
029import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
030import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.isEmpty;
031import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
032import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.Constants.*;
033
034import java.lang.annotation.*;
035import java.lang.reflect.*;
036import java.math.*;
037import java.util.*;
038import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
039import java.util.function.*;
040import java.util.regex.*;
041
042import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.commons.BasicRuntimeException;
044import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
048import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
049import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
050import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
051import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
052
053/**
054 * Represents an OpenAPI schema definition.
055 *
056 * <p>
057 * The schema definition can be applied to any HTTP parts such as bodies, headers, query/form parameters, and URL path parts.
058 * <br>The API is generic enough to apply to any path part although some attributes may only applicable for certain parts.
059 *
060 * <p>
061 * Schema objects are created via builders instantiated through the {@link #create()} method.
062 *
063 * <h5 class='section'>Jakarta Bean Validation Support:</h5>
064 * <p>
065 * As of 9.2.0, this class supports Jakarta Bean Validation constraint annotations (e.g., <c>@NotNull</c>, <c>@Size</c>, <c>@Min</c>, <c>@Max</c>).
066 * When these annotations are encountered during schema building, they are automatically mapped to corresponding OpenAPI schema properties:
067 * <ul>
068 *    <li><c>@NotNull</c> → <c>required(true)</c>
069 *    <li><c>@Size(min=x, max=y)</c> → <c>minLength/maxLength</c> and <c>minItems/maxItems</c>
070 *    <li><c>@Min(value)</c> → <c>minimum(value)</c>
071 *    <li><c>@Max(value)</c> → <c>maximum(value)</c>
072 *    <li><c>@Pattern(regexp)</c> → <c>pattern(regexp)</c>
073 *    <li><c>@Email</c> → <c>format("email")</c>
074 *    <li><c>@Positive/@PositiveOrZero/@Negative/@NegativeOrZero</c> → Corresponding min/max constraints
075 *    <li><c>@NotEmpty</c> → <c>required(true) + minLength(1)/minItems(1)</c>
076 *    <li><c>@NotBlank</c> → <c>required(true) + minLength(1) + pattern</c>
077 *    <li><c>@DecimalMin/@DecimalMax</c> → <c>minimum/maximum</c> with optional <c>exclusiveMinimum/exclusiveMaximum</c>
078 * </ul>
079 * <p>
080 * This integration uses pure reflection and does not require <c>jakarta.validation-api</c> as a dependency.
081 * The annotations are detected and processed automatically when present.
082 *
083 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
084 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
085 * </ul>
086 *
087 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
088 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/OpenApiSupport">OpenApi Basics</a>
089 * </ul>
090 */
091@SuppressWarnings({
092   "java:S116", // Field names use trailing underscores (default_, enum_, const_) to avoid Java keyword conflicts
093   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_additionalProperties)
094   "java:S5843", // Complex regex patterns needed for RFC compliance (IPv6, ISO 8601 duration, date-time formats)
095   "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required for parsed type metadata
096})
097public class HttpPartSchema {
098
099   private static final AnnotationProvider AP = AnnotationProvider.INSTANCE;
100
101   // Property name constants
102   private static final String PROP_additionalProperties = "additionalProperties";
103   private static final String PROP_allowEmptyValue = "allowEmptyValue";
104   private static final String PROP_collectionFormat = "collectionFormat";
105   private static final String PROP_default = "default";
106   private static final String PROP_enum = "enum";
107   private static final String PROP_exclusiveMaximum = "exclusiveMaximum";
108   private static final String PROP_exclusiveMinimum = "exclusiveMinimum";
109   private static final String PROP_format = "format";
110   private static final String PROP_items = "items";
111   private static final String PROP_maximum = "maximum";
112   private static final String PROP_maxItems = "maxItems";
113   private static final String PROP_maxLength = "maxLength";
114   private static final String PROP_maxProperties = "maxProperties";
115   private static final String PROP_minimum = "minimum";
116   private static final String PROP_minItems = "minItems";
117   private static final String PROP_minLength = "minLength";
118   private static final String PROP_minProperties = "minProperties";
119   private static final String PROP_multipleOf = "multipleOf";
120   private static final String PROP_name = "name";
121   private static final String PROP_parsedType = "parsedType";
122   private static final String PROP_pattern = "pattern";
123   private static final String PROP_properties = "properties";
124   private static final String PROP_required = "required";
125   private static final String PROP_skipIfEmpty = "skipIfEmpty";
126   private static final String PROP_type = "type";
127   private static final String PROP_uniqueItems = "uniqueItems";
128
129   // Jakarta validation annotation class name constants
130   private static final String CLASSNAME_NotNull = "NotNull";
131   private static final String CLASSNAME_Size = "Size";
132   private static final String CLASSNAME_Min = "Min";
133   private static final String CLASSNAME_Max = "Max";
134   private static final String CLASSNAME_Pattern = "Pattern";
135   private static final String CLASSNAME_Email = "Email";
136   private static final String CLASSNAME_Positive = "Positive";
137   private static final String CLASSNAME_PositiveOrZero = "PositiveOrZero";
138   private static final String CLASSNAME_Negative = "Negative";
139   private static final String CLASSNAME_NegativeOrZero = "NegativeOrZero";
140   private static final String CLASSNAME_NotEmpty = "NotEmpty";
141   private static final String CLASSNAME_NotBlank = "NotBlank";
142   private static final String CLASSNAME_DecimalMin = "DecimalMin";
143   private static final String CLASSNAME_DecimalMax = "DecimalMax";
144
145   // Annotation attribute name constants
146   private static final String ANN_value = "value";
147   private static final String ANN_min = "min";
148   private static final String ANN_max = "max";
149   private static final String ANN_regexp = "regexp";
150   private static final String ANN_inclusive = "inclusive";
151
152   /**
153    * Builder class.
154    */
155   @SuppressWarnings({
156      "java:S116" // Field names intentionally match JSON property names
157   })
158   public static class Builder {
159      String name;
160      String default_;
161      Set<Integer> codes;
162      Set<String> enum_;
163      Boolean allowEmptyValue;
164      Boolean exclusiveMaximum;
165      Boolean exclusiveMinimum;
166      Boolean required;
167      Boolean uniqueItems;
168      Boolean skipIfEmpty;
169      HttpPartCollectionFormat collectionFormat = HttpPartCollectionFormat.NO_COLLECTION_FORMAT;
170      HttpPartDataType type = HttpPartDataType.NO_TYPE;
171      HttpPartFormat format = HttpPartFormat.NO_FORMAT;
172      Pattern pattern;
173      Number maximum;
174      Number minimum;
175      Number multipleOf;
176      Long maxLength;
177      Long minLength;
178      Long maxItems;
179      Long minItems;
180      Long maxProperties;
181      Long minProperties;
182      Map<String,Object> properties;
183      Object items;
184      Object additionalProperties;
185      boolean noValidate;
186      Class<?> parser;
187      Class<?> serializer;
188      // JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 properties
189      String const_;
190      String[] examples;
191      Boolean deprecated;
192      Number exclusiveMaximumValue;
193      Number exclusiveMinimumValue;
194
195      /**
196       * <mk>const</mk> field (JSON Schema Draft 2020-12).
197       *
198       * <p>
199       * Defines a constant value for this schema.
200       * The instance must be equal to this value to validate.
201       *
202       * @param value
203       *    The new value for this property.
204       * @return This object.
205       */
206      @SuppressWarnings({
207         "java:S100" // Method name uses underscore suffix to match JSON Schema keyword
208      })
209      public Builder const_(String value) {
210         const_ = value;
211         return this;
212      }
213
214      /**
215       * <mk>default</mk> field.
216       *
217       * <p>
218       * Declares the value of the parameter that the server will use if none is provided, for example a "count" to control the number of results per page might default to 100 if not supplied by the client in the request.
219       * <br>(Note: "default" has no meaning for required parameters.)
220       *
221       * <p>
222       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
223       * <ul>
224       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
225       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
226       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
227       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#headerObject">Header</a>
228       * </ul>
229       *
230       * @param value
231       *    The new value for this property.
232       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk>.
233       * @return This object.
234       */
235      @SuppressWarnings({
236         "java:S100" // Method name uses underscore suffix to avoid Java keyword conflict
237      })
238      public Builder default_(String value) {
239         if (ine(value))
240            default_ = value;
241         return this;
242      }
243
244      /**
245       * <mk>enum</mk> field.
246       *
247       * <p>
248       * If specified, the input validates successfully if it is equal to one of the elements in this array.
249       *
250       * <p>
251       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
252       * <ul>
253       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
254       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
255       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
256       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#headerObject">Header</a>
257       * </ul>
258       *
259       * @param value
260       *    The new value for this property.
261       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or an empty set.
262       * @return This object.
263       */
264      @SuppressWarnings({
265         "java:S100" // Method name uses underscore suffix to avoid Java keyword conflict
266      })
267      public Builder enum_(Set<String> value) {
268         if (nn(value) && ! value.isEmpty())
269            enum_ = value;
270         return this;
271      }
272
273      /**
274       * <mk>enum</mk> field.
275       *
276       * <p>
277       * Same as {@link #enum_(Set)} but takes in a var-args array.
278       *
279       * @param values
280       *    The new values for this property.
281       *    <br>Ignored if value is empty.
282       * @return This object.
283       */
284      @SuppressWarnings({
285         "java:S100" // Method name uses underscore suffix to avoid Java keyword conflict
286      })
287      public Builder enum_(String...values) {
288         return enum_(set(values));
289      }
290
291      /**
292       * <mk>additionalProperties</mk> field.
293       *
294       * <p>
295       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
296       * <ul>
297       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
298       * </ul>
299       *
300       * @param value
301       *    The new value for this property.
302       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
303       * @return This object.
304       */
305      public Builder additionalProperties(Builder value) {
306         if (nn(value))
307            additionalProperties = value;
308         return this;
309      }
310
311      /**
312       * <mk>additionalProperties</mk> field.
313       *
314       * <p>
315       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
316       * <ul>
317       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
318       * </ul>
319       *
320       * @param value
321       *    The new value for this property.
322       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
323       * @return This object.
324       */
325      public Builder additionalProperties(HttpPartSchema value) {
326         if (nn(value))
327            additionalProperties = value;
328         return this;
329      }
330
331      /**
332       * Synonym for {@link #allowEmptyValue()}.
333       *
334       * @return This object.
335       */
336      public Builder aev() {
337         return allowEmptyValue(true);
338      }
339
340      /**
341       * Synonym for {@link #allowEmptyValue(Boolean)}.
342       *
343       * @param value
344       *    The new value for this property.
345       * @return This object.
346       */
347      public Builder aev(Boolean value) {
348         return allowEmptyValue(value);
349      }
350
351      /**
352       * Synonym for {@link #allowEmptyValue(String)}.
353       *
354       * @param value
355       *    The new value for this property.
356       * @return This object.
357       */
358      public Builder aev(String value) {
359         return allowEmptyValue(value);
360      }
361
362      /**
363       * <mk>allowEmptyValue</mk> field.
364       *
365       * <p>
366       * Shortcut for calling <code>allowEmptyValue(<jk>true</jk>);</code>.
367       *
368       * @return This object.
369       */
370      public Builder allowEmptyValue() {
371         return allowEmptyValue(true);
372      }
373
374      /**
375       * <mk>allowEmptyValue</mk> field.
376       *
377       * <p>
378       * Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
379       * <br>This is valid only for either query or formData parameters and allows you to send a parameter with a name only or an empty value.
380       * <br>The default value is <jk>false</jk>.
381       *
382       * <p>
383       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
384       * <ul>
385       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
386       * </ul>
387       *
388       * @param value
389       *    The new value for this property.
390       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk>.
391       * @return This object.
392       */
393      public Builder allowEmptyValue(Boolean value) {
394         allowEmptyValue = resolve(value, allowEmptyValue);
395         return this;
396      }
397
398      /**
399       * <mk>allowEmptyValue</mk> field.
400       *
401       * <p>
402       * Same as {@link #allowEmptyValue(Boolean)} but takes in a string boolean value.
403       *
404       * @param value
405       *    The new value for this property.
406       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
407       * @return This object.
408       */
409      public Builder allowEmptyValue(String value) {
410         allowEmptyValue = resolve(value, allowEmptyValue);
411         return this;
412      }
413
414      /**
415       * Shortcut for <c>additionalProperties(value)</c>
416       *
417       * <p>
418       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
419       * <ul>
420       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
421       * </ul>
422       *
423       * @param value
424       *    The new value for this property.
425       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
426       * @return This object.
427       */
428      public Builder ap(Builder value) {
429         return additionalProperties(value);
430      }
431
432      /**
433       * Shortcut for <c>additionalProperties(value)</c>
434       *
435       * <p>
436       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
437       * <ul>
438       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
439       * </ul>
440       *
441       * @param value
442       *    The new value for this property.
443       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
444       * @return This object.
445       */
446      public Builder ap(HttpPartSchema value) {
447         return additionalProperties(value);
448      }
449
450      /**
451       * Apply the specified annotation to this schema.
452       *
453       * @param a The annotation to apply.
454       * @return This object.
455      */
456      public Builder apply(Annotation a) {
457         if (a instanceof Schema a2)
458            return apply(a2);
459         if (a instanceof Items a2)
460            return apply(a2);
461         if (a instanceof SubItems a2)
462            return apply(a2);
463         if (a instanceof HttpPartMarshalling a2)
464            return apply(a2);
465         if (cn(a.annotationType()).startsWith("jakarta.validation.constraints.")) {
466            applyJakartaValidation(a);
467            return this;
468         }
469         return applyHttpAnnotation(a);
470      }
471
472      /**
473       * Instantiates a new {@link HttpPartSchema} object based on the configuration of this builder.
474       *
475       * <p>
476       * This method can be called multiple times to produce new schema objects.
477       *
478       * @return
479       *    A new {@link HttpPartSchema} object.
480       *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
481       */
482      public HttpPartSchema build() {
483         return new HttpPartSchema(this);
484      }
485
486      /**
487       * Synonym for {@link #collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat)}.
488       *
489       * @param value
490       *    The new value for this property.
491       * @return This object.
492       */
493      public Builder cf(HttpPartCollectionFormat value) {
494         return collectionFormat(value);
495      }
496
497      /**
498       * Synonym for {@link #collectionFormat(String)}.
499       *
500       * @param value
501       *    The new value for this property.
502       * @return This object.
503       */
504      public Builder cf(String value) {
505         return collectionFormat(value);
506      }
507
508      /**
509       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.CSV)</c>.
510       *
511       * @return This object.
512       */
513      public Builder cfCsv() {
514         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.CSV);
515      }
516
517      /**
518       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.MULTI)</c>.
519       *
520       * @return This object.
521       */
522      public Builder cfMulti() {
523         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.MULTI);
524      }
525
526      /**
527       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.NO_COLLECTION_FORMAT)</c>.
528       *
529       * @return This object.
530       */
531      public Builder cfNone() {
532         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.NO_COLLECTION_FORMAT);
533      }
534
535      /**
536       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.PIPES)</c>.
537       *
538       * @return This object.
539       */
540      public Builder cfPipes() {
541         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.PIPES);
542      }
543
544      /**
545       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.SSV)</c>.
546       *
547       * @return This object.
548       */
549      public Builder cfSsv() {
550         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.SSV);
551      }
552
553      /**
554       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.TSV)</c>.
555       *
556       * @return This object.
557       */
558      public Builder cfTsv() {
559         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.TSV);
560      }
561
562      /**
563       * Shortcut for <c>collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.UONC)</c>.
564       *
565       * @return This object.
566       */
567      public Builder cfUon() {
568         return collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.UONC);
569      }
570
571      /**
572       * <mk>httpStatusCode</mk> key.
573       *
574       * <p>
575       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
576       * <ul>
577       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#responsesObject">Responses</a>
578       * </ul>
579       *
580       * @param value
581       *    The new value for this property.
582       *    <br>Ignored if value is <c>0</c>.
583       * @return This object.
584       */
585      public Builder code(int value) {
586         if (value != 0) {
587            if (codes == null)
588               codes = new TreeSet<>();
589            codes.add(value);
590         }
591         return this;
592      }
593
594      /**
595       * <mk>httpStatusCode</mk> key.
596       *
597       * <p>
598       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
599       * <ul>
600       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#responsesObject">Responses</a>
601       * </ul>
602       *
603       * @param value
604       *    The new value for this property.
605       *    <br>Ignored if <jk>null</jk> or an empty array.
606       * @return This object.
607       */
608      public Builder codes(int[] value) {
609         if (nn(value) && value.length != 0)
610            for (var v : value)
611               code(v);
612         return this;
613      }
614
615      /**
616       * <mk>collectionFormat</mk> field.
617       *
618       * <p>
619       * Determines the format of the array if <c>type</c> <js>"array"</js> is used.
620       * <br>Can only be used if <c>type</c> is <js>"array"</js>.
621       *
622       * <p>
623       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
624       * <ul>
625       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
626       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
627       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#headerObject">Header</a>
628       * </ul>
629       *
630       * <p>
631       * Note that for collections/arrays parameters with POJO element types, the input is broken into a string array before being converted into POJO elements.
632       *
633       * <ul class='values javatree'>
634       *    <ul class='jc'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat}
635       *    <ul>
636       *       <li>
637       *          {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#CSV CSV} (default) - Comma-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo,bar"</js>).
638       *       <li>
639       *          {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#SSV SSV} - Space-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo bar"</js>).
640       *       <li>
641       *          {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#TSV TSV} - Tab-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo\tbar"</js>).
642       *       <li>
643       *          {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#PIPES PIPES} - Pipe-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo|bar"</js>).
644       *       <li>
645       *          {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#MULTI MULTI} - Corresponds to multiple parameter instances instead of multiple values for a single instance (e.g. <js>"foo=bar&amp;foo=baz"</js>).
646       *       <li>
647       *          {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#UONC UONC} - UON collection notation (e.g. <js>"@(foo,bar)"</js>).
648       *    </ul>
649       * </ul>
650       *
651       * @param value
652       *    The new value for this property.
653       * @return This object.
654       */
655      public Builder collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat value) {
656         collectionFormat = value;
657         return this;
658      }
659
660      /**
661       * <mk>collectionFormat</mk> field.
662       *
663       * <p>
664       * Determines the format of the array if <c>type</c> <js>"array"</js> is used.
665       * <br>Can only be used if <c>type</c> is <js>"array"</js>.
666       *
667       * <p>
668       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
669       * <ul>
670       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
671       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
672       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#headerObject">Header</a>
673       * </ul>
674       *
675       * <p>
676       * Note that for collections/arrays parameters with POJO element types, the input is broken into a string array before being converted into POJO elements.
677       *
678       * <ul class='values'>
679       *    <li>
680       *       <js>"csv"</js> (default) - Comma-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo,bar"</js>).
681       *    <li>
682       *       <js>"ssv"</js> - Space-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo bar"</js>).
683       *    <li>
684       *       <js>"tsv"</js> - Tab-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo\tbar"</js>).
685       *    <li>
686       *       <js>"pipes</js> - Pipe-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo|bar"</js>).
687       *    <li>
688       *       <js>"multi"</js> - Corresponds to multiple parameter instances instead of multiple values for a single instance (e.g. <js>"foo=bar&amp;foo=baz"</js>).
689       *    <li>
690       *       <js>"uon"</js> - UON notation (e.g. <js>"@(foo,bar)"</js>).
691       *    <li>
692       * </ul>
693       *
694       * @param value
695       *    The new value for this property.
696       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
697       * @return This object.
698       */
699      public Builder collectionFormat(String value) {
700         try {
701            if (ine(value))
702               this.collectionFormat = HttpPartCollectionFormat.fromString(value);
703         } catch (Exception e) {
704            throw new BasicRuntimeException(e, "Invalid value ''{0}'' passed in as collectionFormat value.  Valid values: {1}", value, HttpPartCollectionFormat.values());
705         }
706         return this;
707      }

1112      /**
1113       * <mk>format</mk> field.
1114       *
1115       * <p>
1116       * The extending format for the previously mentioned <a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterType">parameter type</a>.
1117       *
1118       * <p>
1119       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
1120       * <ul>
1121       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
1122       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
1123       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
1124       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#headerObject">Header</a>
1125       * </ul>
1126       *
1127       * <ul class='values javatree'>
1128       *    <ul class='jc'>{@link HttpPartFormat}
1129       *    <ul>
1130       *       <li class='jf'>
1131       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#INT32 INT32} - Signed 32 bits.
1132       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"integer"</js>.
1133       *       <li class='jf'>
1134       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#INT64 INT64} - Signed 64 bits.
1135       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"integer"</js>.
1136       *       <li class='jf'>
1137       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#FLOAT FLOAT} - 32-bit floating point number.
1138       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"number"</js>.
1139       *       <li class='jf'>
1140       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#DOUBLE DOUBLE} - 64-bit floating point number.
1141       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"number"</js>.
1142       *       <li class='jf'>
1143       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#BYTE BYTE} - BASE-64 encoded characters.
1144       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1145       *          <br>Parameters of type POJO convertible from string are converted after the string has been decoded.
1146       *       <li class='jf'>
1147       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#BINARY BINARY} - Hexadecimal encoded octets (e.g. <js>"00FF"</js>).
1148       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1149       *          <br>Parameters of type POJO convertible from string are converted after the string has been decoded.
1150       *       <li class='jf'>
1151       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#BINARY_SPACED BINARY_SPACED} - Hexadecimal encoded octets, spaced (e.g. <js>"00 FF"</js>).
1152       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1153       *          <br>Parameters of type POJO convertible from string are converted after the string has been decoded.
1154       *       <li class='jf'>
1155       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#DATE DATE} - An <a href='http://xml2rfc.ietf.org/public/rfc/html/rfc3339.html#anchor14'>RFC3339 full-date</a>.
1156       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1157       *       <li class='jf'>
1158       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#DATE_TIME DATE_TIME} - An <a href='http://xml2rfc.ietf.org/public/rfc/html/rfc3339.html#anchor14'>RFC3339 date-time</a>.
1159       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1160       *       <li class='jf'>
1161       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#PASSWORD PASSWORD} - Used to hint UIs the input needs to be obscured.
1162       *          <br>This format does not affect the serialization or parsing of the parameter.
1163       *       <li class='jf'>
1164       *          {@link HttpPartFormat#UON UON} - UON notation (e.g. <js>"(foo=bar,baz=@(qux,123))"</js>).
1165       *          <br>Only valid with type <js>"object"</js>.
1166       *          <br>If not specified, then the input is interpreted as plain-text and is converted to a POJO directly.
1167       *    </ul>
1168       * </ul>
1169       *
1170       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1171       *    <li class='extlink'><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Swagger Schema Object</a>
1172       * </ul>
1173       *
1174       * @param value
1175       *    The new value for this property.
1176       * @return This object.
1177       */
1178      public Builder format(HttpPartFormat value) {
1179         format = value;
1180         return this;
1181      }
1182
1183      /**
1184       * <mk>format</mk> field.
1185       *
1186       * <p>
1187       * The extending format for the previously mentioned <a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterType">parameter type</a>.
1188       *
1189       * <p>
1190       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
1191       * <ul>
1192       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
1193       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
1194       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
1195       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#headerObject">Header</a>
1196       * </ul>
1197       *
1198       * <ul class='values'>
1199       *    <li>
1200       *       <js>"int32"</js> - Signed 32 bits.
1201       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"integer"</js>.
1202       *    <li>
1203       *       <js>"int64"</js> - Signed 64 bits.
1204       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"integer"</js>.
1205       *    <li>
1206       *       <js>"float"</js> - 32-bit floating point number.
1207       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"number"</js>.
1208       *    <li>
1209       *       <js>"double"</js> - 64-bit floating point number.
1210       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"number"</js>.
1211       *    <li>
1212       *       <js>"byte"</js> - BASE-64 encoded characters.
1213       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1214       *       <br>Parameters of type POJO convertible from string are converted after the string has been decoded.
1215       *    <li>
1216       *       <js>"binary"</js> - Hexadecimal encoded octets (e.g. <js>"00FF"</js>).
1217       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1218       *       <br>Parameters of type POJO convertible from string are converted after the string has been decoded.
1219       *    <li>
1220       *       <js>"binary-spaced"</js> - Hexadecimal encoded octets, spaced (e.g. <js>"00 FF"</js>).
1221       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1222       *       <br>Parameters of type POJO convertible from string are converted after the string has been decoded.
1223       *    <li>
1224       *       <js>"date"</js> - An <a href='http://xml2rfc.ietf.org/public/rfc/html/rfc3339.html#anchor14'>RFC3339 full-date</a>.
1225       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1226       *    <li>
1227       *       <js>"date-time"</js> - An <a href='http://xml2rfc.ietf.org/public/rfc/html/rfc3339.html#anchor14'>RFC3339 date-time</a>.
1228       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"string"</js>.
1229       *    <li>
1230       *       <js>"password"</js> - Used to hint UIs the input needs to be obscured.
1231       *       <br>This format does not affect the serialization or parsing of the parameter.
1232       *    <li>
1233       *       <js>"uon"</js> - UON notation (e.g. <js>"(foo=bar,baz=@(qux,123))"</js>).
1234       *       <br>Only valid with type <js>"object"</js>.
1235       *       <br>If not specified, then the input is interpreted as plain-text and is converted to a POJO directly.
1236       * </ul>
1237       *
1238       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1239       *    <li class='extlink'><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Swagger Schema Object</a>
1240       * </ul>

1244       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or an empty string.
1245       * @return This object.
1246       */
1247      public Builder format(String value) {
1248         try {
1249            if (ine(value))
1250               format = HttpPartFormat.fromString(value);
1251         } catch (Exception e) {
1252            throw new BasicRuntimeException(e, "Invalid value ''{0}'' passed in as format value.  Valid values: {1}", value, HttpPartFormat.values());
1253         }
1254         return this;
1255      }

1288      /**

2336      /**
2337       * <mk>type</mk> field.
2338       *
2339       * <p>
2340       * The type of the parameter.
2341       *
2342       * <p>
2343       * If the type is not specified, it will be auto-detected based on the parameter class type.
2344       *
2345       * <p>
2346       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
2347       * <ul>
2348       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
2349       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
2350       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
2351       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#securitySchemeObject">SecurityScheme</a>
2352       * </ul>
2353       *
2354       * <ul class='values javatree'>
2355       *    <li class='jc'>{@link HttpPartDataType}
2356       *    <ul>
2357       *       <li class='jf'>
2358       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#STRING STRING}
2359       *          <br>Parameter must be a string or a POJO convertible from a string.
2360       *          <li>
2361       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#NUMBER NUMBER}
2362       *          <br>Parameter must be a number primitive or number object.
2363       *          <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates either a <c>Float</c> or <c>Double</c> depending on the size of the number.
2364       *       <li class='jf'>
2365       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#INTEGER INTEGER}
2366       *          <br>Parameter must be a integer/long primitive or integer/long object.
2367       *          <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates either a <c>Short</c>, <c>Integer</c>, or <c>Long</c> depending on the size of the number.
2368       *       <li class='jf'>
2369       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#BOOLEAN BOOLEAN}
2370       *          <br>Parameter must be a boolean primitive or object.
2371       *       <li class='jf'>
2372       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#ARRAY ARRAY}
2373       *          <br>Parameter must be an array or collection.
2374       *          <br>Elements must be strings or POJOs convertible from strings.
2375       *          <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates an {@link JsonList}.
2376       *       <li class='jf'>
2377       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#OBJECT OBJECT}
2378       *          <br>Parameter must be a map or bean.
2379       *          <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates a map.
2380       *          <br>Note that this is an extension of the OpenAPI schema as Juneau allows for arbitrarily-complex POJOs to be serialized as HTTP parts.
2381       *       <li class='jf'>
2382       *          {@link HttpPartDataType#FILE FILE}
2383       *          <br>This type is currently not supported.
2384       *    </ul>
2385       * </ul>
2386       *
2387       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2388       *    <li class='extlink'><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification#dataTypes">Swagger Data Types</a>
2389       * </ul>
2390       *
2391       * @param value
2392       *    The new value for this property.
2393       * @return This object.
2394       */
2395      public Builder type(HttpPartDataType value) {
2396         type = value;
2397         return this;
2398      }
2399
2400      /**
2401       * <mk>type</mk> field.
2402       *
2403       * <p>
2404       * The type of the parameter.
2405       *
2406       * <p>
2407       * If the type is not specified, it will be auto-detected based on the parameter class type.
2408       *
2409       * <p>
2410       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
2411       * <ul>
2412       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>
2413       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#schemaObject">Schema</a>
2414       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#itemsObject">Items</a>
2415       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#securitySchemeObject">SecurityScheme</a>
2416       * </ul>
2417       *
2418       * <ul class='values'>
2419       *    <li>
2420       *       <js>"string"</js>
2421       *       <br>Parameter must be a string or a POJO convertible from a string.
2422       *    <li>
2423       *       <js>"number"</js>
2424       *       <br>Parameter must be a number primitive or number object.
2425       *       <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates either a <c>Float</c> or <c>Double</c> depending on the size of the number.
2426       *    <li>
2427       *       <js>"integer"</js>
2428       *       <br>Parameter must be a integer/long primitive or integer/long object.
2429       *       <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates either a <c>Short</c>, <c>Integer</c>, or <c>Long</c> depending on the size of the number.
2430       *    <li>
2431       *       <js>"boolean"</js>
2432       *       <br>Parameter must be a boolean primitive or object.
2433       *    <li>
2434       *       <js>"array"</js>
2435       *       <br>Parameter must be an array or collection.
2436       *       <br>Elements must be strings or POJOs convertible from strings.
2437       *       <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates an {@link JsonList}.
2438       *    <li>
2439       *       <js>"object"</js>
2440       *       <br>Parameter must be a map or bean.
2441       *       <br>If parameter is <c>Object</c>, creates a map.
2442       *       <br>Note that this is an extension of the OpenAPI schema as Juneau allows for arbitrarily-complex POJOs to be serialized as HTTP parts.
2443       *    <li>
2444       *       <js>"file"</js>
2445       *       <br>This type is currently not supported.
2446       * </ul>
2447       *
2448       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2449       *    <li class='extlink'><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification#dataTypes">Swagger Data Types</a>
2450       * </ul>
2451       *
2452       * @param value
2453       *    The new value for this property.
2454       *    <br>Ignored if value is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
2455       * @return This object.
2456       */
2457      public Builder type(String value) {
2458         try {
2459            if (ine(value))
2460               type = HttpPartDataType.fromString(value);
2461         } catch (Exception e) {
2462            throw new BasicRuntimeException(e, "Invalid value ''{0}'' passed in as type value.  Valid values: {1}", value, HttpPartDataType.values());
2463         }
2464         return this;
2465      }

2510      /**

2513       * <p>
2514       * If <jk>true</jk>, the input validates successfully if all of its elements are unique.
2515       *
2516       * <p>
2517       * <br>If the parameter type is a subclass of {@link Set}, this validation is skipped (since a set can only contain unique items anyway).
2518       * <br>Otherwise, the collection or array is checked for duplicate items.
2519       *
2520       * <p>
2521       * Only allowed for the following types: <js>"array"</js>.
2522       *
2523       * <p>
2524       * Applicable to the following Swagger schema objects:
2525       * <ul>
2526       *    <li><a class="doclink" href="https://swagger.io/specification/v2#parameterObject">Parameter</a>

2558      private static Long firstNmo(Long...l) {
2559         for (var ll : l)
2560            if (nn(ll) && ll != -1)
2561               return ll;
2562         return null;
2563      }
2564
2565      /**
2566       * Helper method to safely get annotation attribute values via reflection.
2567       *
2568       * <p>
2569       * This allows reading Jakarta Validation annotations without requiring them on the classpath.
2570       *
2571       * @param <T> The expected return type.
2572       * @param a The annotation to read from.
2573       * @param attributeName The attribute name to read.
2574       * @param type The expected type of the attribute value.
2575       * @return The attribute value, or <jk>null</jk> if not found or not of the expected type.
2576       */
2577      private static <T> T getAnnotationValue(Annotation a, String attributeName, Class<T> type) {
2578         try {
2579            Method m = a.annotationType().getDeclaredMethod(attributeName);
2580            Object value = m.invoke(a);
2581            return type.isInstance(value) ? type.cast(value) : null;
2582         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception e) {
2583            return null;
2584         }
2585      }
2586
2587      private static String joinnlOrNull(String[]...s) {
2588         for (var ss : s)
2589            if (ss.length > 0)
2590               return StringUtils.joinnl(ss);
2591         return null;
2592      }
2593
2594      private static Boolean resolve(Boolean newValue, Boolean oldValue) {
2595         return newValue == null ? oldValue : newValue;
2596      }
2597
2598      private static Long resolve(Long newValue, Long oldValue) {
2599         return (newValue == null || newValue == -1) ? oldValue : newValue;
2600      }
2601
2602      private static Boolean resolve(String newValue, Boolean oldValue) {
2603         return isEmpty(newValue) ? oldValue : b(newValue);
2604      }
2605
2606      private static Long resolve(String newValue, Long oldValue) {
2607         return isEmpty(newValue) ? oldValue : Long.parseLong(newValue);
2608      }
2609
2610      private static Number toNumber(String...s) {
2611         return HttpPartSchema.toNumber(s);
2612      }
2613
2614      private static Set<String> toSet(String[]...s) {
2615         return HttpPartSchema.toSet(s);
2616      }
2617
2618      Builder apply(Class<? extends Annotation> c, Type t) {
2619         if (t instanceof Class<?> c2) {
2620            rstream(AP.find(c, info(c2))).forEach(x -> apply(x.inner()));
2621         } else if (Holder.isType(t)) {
2622            apply(c, Holder.getParameterType(t));
2623         }
2624         return this;
2625      }
2626
2627      Builder apply(Class<? extends Annotation> c, Method m) {
2628         apply(c, m.getGenericReturnType());
2629         Annotation a = m.getAnnotation(c);
2630         if (nn(a))
2631            return apply(a);
2632         return this;
2633      }
2634
2635      Builder apply(Class<? extends Annotation> c, ParameterInfo mpi) {
2636         apply(c, mpi.getParameterType().innerType());
2637         mpi.getAnnotations(c).forEach(x -> apply(x.inner()));
2638         return this;
2639      }
2640
2641      Builder apply(HttpPartMarshalling a) {
2642         parser(a.parser());
2643         serializer(a.serializer());
2644         return this;
2645      }
2646
2647      private Builder applyHttpAnnotation(Annotation a) {
2648         var annotationName = a.annotationType().getSimpleName();
2649
2650         var schema = getAnnotationValue(a, "schema", Schema.class);
2651         if (schema != null && ("Response".equals(annotationName) || !SchemaAnnotation.empty(schema)))
2652            apply(schema);
2653
2654         var aName = getAnnotationString(a, "name");
2655         var aValue = getAnnotationString(a, ANN_value);
2656
2657         switch (annotationName) {
2658            case "PathRemainder" -> {
2659               name("/*");
2660               default_(getAnnotationString(a, "def"));
2661               allowEmptyValue();
2662               required(false);
2663            }
2664            case "Path" -> {
2665               name(firstNonEmpty(aName, aValue));
2666               var def = getAnnotationString(a, "def");
2667               if (def != null && neq(NONE, def))
2668                  default_ = def;
2669               if (startsWith(name, '/')) {
2670                  allowEmptyValue();
2671                  required(false);
2672               } else if (required == null) {
2673                  required(def == null || eq(NONE, def));
2674               }
2675            }
2676            case "Response" -> {
2677               allowEmptyValue(true);
2678               required(false);
2679            }
2680            case "StatusCode" -> codes(getAnnotationIntArray(a, ANN_value));
2681            default -> {
2682               name(firstNonEmpty(aName, aValue));
2683               default_(getAnnotationString(a, "def"));
2684            }
2685         }
2686         return this;
2687      }
2688
2689      private static String getAnnotationString(Annotation a, String methodName) {
2690         try {
2691            var m = a.annotationType().getMethod(methodName);
2692            if (m.getReturnType() == String.class)
2693               return (String) m.invoke(a);
2694         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NoSuchMethodException e) {
2695            // Attribute doesn't exist on this annotation -- ignore
2696         } catch (Exception e) {
2697            throw rex(e);
2698         }
2699         return null;
2700      }
2701
2702      private static int[] getAnnotationIntArray(Annotation a, String methodName) {
2703         try {
2704            var m = a.annotationType().getMethod(methodName);
2705            if (m.getReturnType() == int[].class) {
2706               var arr = (int[]) m.invoke(a);
2707               return arr == null ? new int[0] : arr;
2708            }
2709         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NoSuchMethodException e) {
2710            // Attribute doesn't exist on this annotation -- ignore
2711         } catch (Exception e) {
2712            throw rex(e);
2713         }
2714         return new int[0];
2715      }
2716
2717      Builder apply(Items a) {
2718         default_(joinnlOrNull(a.default_(), a.df()));
2719         enum_(toSet(a.enum_(), a.e()));
2720         collectionFormat(firstNonEmpty(a.collectionFormat(), a.cf()));
2721         exclusiveMaximum(a.exclusiveMaximum() || a.emax());
2722         exclusiveMinimum(a.exclusiveMinimum() || a.emin());
2723         format(firstNonEmpty(a.format(), a.f()));
2724         items(a.items());
2725         maximum(toNumber(a.maximum(), a.max()));
2726         maxItems(firstNmo(a.maxItems(), a.maxi()));
2727         maxLength(firstNmo(a.maxLength(), a.maxl()));
2728         minimum(toNumber(a.minimum(), a.min()));
2729         minItems(firstNmo(a.minItems(), a.mini()));
2730         minLength(firstNmo(a.minLength(), a.minl()));
2731         multipleOf(toNumber(a.multipleOf(), a.mo()));
2732         pattern(firstNonEmpty(a.pattern(), a.p()));
2733         type(firstNonEmpty(a.type(), a.t()));
2734         uniqueItems(a.uniqueItems() || a.ui());
2735         return this;
2736      }
2737
2738      // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2739      // JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 property setters
2740      // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2741
2742      // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2743      // Other
2744      // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2745
2746      @SuppressWarnings({
2747         "removal" // Handles deprecated boolean-style exclusiveMaximum/exclusiveMinimum for backward compatibility
2748      })
2749      Builder apply(Schema a) {
2750         default_(joinnlOrNull(a.default_(), a.df()));
2751         enum_(toSet(a.enum_(), a.e()));
2752         allowEmptyValue(a.allowEmptyValue() || a.aev());
2753         collectionFormat(firstNonEmpty(a.collectionFormat(), a.cf()));
2754
2755         // Handle exclusiveMaximum with fallback from Draft 2020-12 to Draft 04
2756         String exMaxVal = a.exclusiveMaximumValue();
2757         if (ine(exMaxVal)) {
2758            exclusiveMaximumValue(toNumber(exMaxVal));
2759         } else if (a.exclusiveMaximum() || a.emax()) {
2760            exclusiveMaximum(true);
2761         }
2762
2763         // Handle exclusiveMinimum with fallback from Draft 2020-12 to Draft 04
2764         String exMinVal = a.exclusiveMinimumValue();
2765         if (ine(exMinVal)) {
2766            exclusiveMinimumValue(toNumber(exMinVal));
2767         } else if (a.exclusiveMinimum() || a.emin()) {
2768            exclusiveMinimum(true);
2769         }
2770
2771         format(firstNonEmpty(a.format(), a.f()));
2772         items(a.items());
2773         maximum(toNumber(a.maximum(), a.max()));
2774         maxItems(firstNmo(a.maxItems(), a.maxi()));
2775         maxLength(firstNmo(a.maxLength(), a.maxl()));
2776         maxProperties(firstNmo(a.maxProperties(), a.maxp()));
2777         minimum(toNumber(a.minimum(), a.min()));
2778         minItems(firstNmo(a.minItems(), a.mini()));
2779         minLength(firstNmo(a.minLength(), a.minl()));
2780         minProperties(firstNmo(a.minProperties(), a.minp()));
2781         multipleOf(toNumber(a.multipleOf(), a.mo()));
2782         pattern(firstNonEmpty(a.pattern(), a.p()));
2783         required(a.required() || a.r());
2784         skipIfEmpty(a.skipIfEmpty() || a.sie());
2785         type(firstNonEmpty(a.type(), a.t()));
2786         uniqueItems(a.uniqueItems() || a.ui());
2787
2788         // JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 properties
2789         const_(joinnlOrNull(a.const_()));
2790         examples(a.examples());
2791         deprecated(a.deprecated_());
2792
2793         return this;
2794      }
2795
2796      Builder apply(SubItems a) {
2797         default_(joinnlOrNull(a.default_(), a.df()));
2798         enum_(toSet(a.enum_(), a.e()));
2799         collectionFormat(firstNonEmpty(a.collectionFormat(), a.cf()));
2800         exclusiveMaximum(a.exclusiveMaximum() || a.emax());
2801         exclusiveMinimum(a.exclusiveMinimum() || a.emin());
2802         format(firstNonEmpty(a.format(), a.f()));
2803         maximum(toNumber(a.maximum(), a.max()));
2804         maxItems(firstNmo(a.maxItems(), a.maxi()));
2805         maxLength(firstNmo(a.maxLength(), a.maxl()));
2806         minimum(toNumber(a.minimum(), a.min()));
2807         minItems(firstNmo(a.minItems(), a.mini()));
2808         minLength(firstNmo(a.minLength(), a.minl()));
2809         multipleOf(toNumber(a.multipleOf(), a.mo()));
2810         pattern(firstNonEmpty(a.pattern(), a.p()));
2811         type(firstNonEmpty(a.type(), a.t()));
2812         uniqueItems(a.uniqueItems() || a.ui());
2813         return this;
2814      }
2815
2816      public Builder applyAll(Class<? extends Annotation> c, Type t) {
2817         return apply(Schema.class, t).apply(c, t);
2818      }
2819
2820      public Builder applyAll(Class<? extends Annotation> c, Method m) {
2821         return apply(Schema.class, m).apply(c, m);
2822      }
2823
2824      public Builder applyAll(Class<? extends Annotation> c, ParameterInfo mpi) {
2825         return apply(Schema.class, mpi).apply(c, mpi);
2826      }
2827
2828      /**
2829       * Apply Jakarta Bean Validation constraints to this schema.
2830       *
2831       * <p>
2832       * This method uses pure reflection to read constraint annotations, so no jakarta.validation-api
2833       * dependency is required. The constraints are mapped to OpenAPI schema properties where applicable.
2834       *
2835       * <p>
2836       * Supported constraints:
2837       * <ul>
2838       *    <li><c>@NotNull</c> → <c>required(true)</c>
2839       *    <li><c>@Size</c> → <c>minLength/maxLength</c> or <c>minItems/maxItems</c>
2840       *    <li><c>@Min</c> → <c>minimum</c>
2841       *    <li><c>@Max</c> → <c>maximum</c>
2842       *    <li><c>@DecimalMin</c> → <c>minimum + exclusiveMinimum</c>
2843       *    <li><c>@DecimalMax</c> → <c>maximum + exclusiveMaximum</c>
2844       *    <li><c>@Pattern</c> → <c>pattern</c>
2845       *    <li><c>@Email</c> → <c>format("email")</c>
2846       *    <li><c>@Positive</c> → <c>minimum(0) + exclusiveMinimum(true)</c>
2847       *    <li><c>@PositiveOrZero</c> → <c>minimum(0)</c>
2848       *    <li><c>@Negative</c> → <c>maximum(0) + exclusiveMaximum(true)</c>
2849       *    <li><c>@NegativeOrZero</c> → <c>maximum(0)</c>
2850       *    <li><c>@NotEmpty</c> → <c>required(true) + minLength(1)/minItems(1)</c>
2851       *    <li><c>@NotBlank</c> → <c>required(true) + minLength(1) + pattern</c>
2852       * </ul>
2853       *
2854       * @param a The Jakarta Validation constraint annotation.
2855       * @return This object.
2856       * @since 9.2.0
2857       */
2858      @SuppressWarnings({
2859         "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for Jakarta validation application
2860         "java:S6541" // Brain method: consolidated Jakarta validation constraint dispatch
2861      })
2862      Builder applyJakartaValidation(Annotation a) {
2863         String simpleName = cns(a.annotationType());
2864
2865         try {
2866            switch (simpleName) {
2867               case CLASSNAME_NotNull:
2868                  required(true);
2869                  break;
2870            case CLASSNAME_Size:
2871               Integer min = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_min, Integer.class);
2872               Integer max = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_max, Integer.class);
2873                  if (nn(min) && min > 0) {
2874                     minLength(min.longValue());
2875                     minItems(min.longValue());
2876                  }
2877                  if (nn(max) && max < Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
2878                     maxLength(max.longValue());
2879                     maxItems(max.longValue());
2880                  }
2881                  break;
2882            case CLASSNAME_Min:
2883               Long minValue = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_value, Long.class);
2884                  if (nn(minValue))
2885                     minimum(minValue);
2886                  break;
2887            case CLASSNAME_Max:
2888               Long maxValue = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_value, Long.class);
2889                  if (nn(maxValue))
2890                     maximum(maxValue);
2891                  break;
2892            case CLASSNAME_Pattern:
2893               String regexp = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_regexp, String.class);
2894                  if (nn(regexp))
2895                     pattern(regexp);
2896                  break;
2897               case CLASSNAME_Email:
2898                  format("email");
2899                  break;
2900               case CLASSNAME_Positive:
2901                  minimum(0);
2902                  exclusiveMinimum(true);
2903                  break;
2904               case CLASSNAME_PositiveOrZero:
2905                  minimum(0);
2906                  break;
2907               case CLASSNAME_Negative:
2908                  maximum(0);
2909                  exclusiveMaximum(true);
2910                  break;
2911               case CLASSNAME_NegativeOrZero:
2912                  maximum(0);
2913                  break;
2914               case CLASSNAME_NotEmpty:
2915                  required(true);
2916                  minLength(1L);
2917                  minItems(1L);
2918                  break;
2919               case CLASSNAME_NotBlank:
2920                  required(true);
2921                  minLength(1L);
2922                  pattern(".*\\S.*"); // Contains at least one non-whitespace character
2923                  break;
2924            case CLASSNAME_DecimalMin:
2925               String minVal = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_value, String.class);
2926               Boolean minInclusive = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_inclusive, Boolean.class);
2927                  if (nn(minVal)) {
2928                     minimum(toNumber(minVal));
2929                     if (isFalse(minInclusive))
2930                        exclusiveMinimum(true);
2931                  }
2932                  break;
2933            case CLASSNAME_DecimalMax:
2934               String maxVal = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_value, String.class);
2935               Boolean maxInclusive = getAnnotationValue(a, ANN_inclusive, Boolean.class);
2936                  if (nn(maxVal)) {
2937                     maximum(toNumber(maxVal));
2938                     if (isFalse(maxInclusive))
2939                        exclusiveMaximum(true);
2940                  }
2941                  break;
2942               default:
2943                  // Silently ignore other validation annotations we don't support yet
2944            }
2945         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception e) {
2946            // If reflection fails, just skip this annotation - it's optional
2947         }
2948         return this;
2949      }
2950
2951      Builder items(Items value) {
2952         if (! ItemsAnnotation.empty(value))
2953            items = HttpPartSchema.create().apply(value);
2954         return this;
2955      }
2956
2957      Builder items(SubItems value) {
2958         if (! SubItemsAnnotation.empty(value))
2959            items = HttpPartSchema.create().apply(value);
2960         return this;
2961      }
2962   }
2963
2964   /** Reusable instance of this object, all default settings. */
2965   public static final HttpPartSchema DEFAULT = HttpPartSchema.create().allowEmptyValue(true).build();
2966
2967   /** Boolean type */
2968   public static final HttpPartSchema T_BOOLEAN = HttpPartSchema.tBoolean().build();
2969
2970   /** File type */
2971   public static final HttpPartSchema T_FILE = HttpPartSchema.tFile().build();
2972
2973   /** Integer type */
2974   public static final HttpPartSchema T_INTEGER = HttpPartSchema.tInteger().build();
2975
2976   /** Int32 type */
2977   public static final HttpPartSchema T_INT32 = HttpPartSchema.tInt32().build();
2978
2979   /** Int64 type */
2980   public static final HttpPartSchema T_INT64 = HttpPartSchema.tInt64().build();
2981
2982   /** No type */
2983   public static final HttpPartSchema T_NONE = HttpPartSchema.tNone().build();
2984
2985   /** Number type */
2986   public static final HttpPartSchema T_NUMBER = HttpPartSchema.tNumber().build();
2987
2988   /** Float type */
2989   public static final HttpPartSchema T_FLOAT = HttpPartSchema.tFloat().build();
2990
2991   /** Double type */
2992   public static final HttpPartSchema T_DOUBLE = HttpPartSchema.tDouble().build();
2993
2994   /** String type */
2995   public static final HttpPartSchema T_STRING = HttpPartSchema.tString().build();
2996
2997   /** Byte type */
2998   public static final HttpPartSchema T_BYTE = HttpPartSchema.tByte().build();
2999
3000   /** Binary type */
3001   public static final HttpPartSchema T_BINARY = HttpPartSchema.tBinary().build();
3002
3003   /** Spaced binary type */
3004   public static final HttpPartSchema T_BINARY_SPACED = HttpPartSchema.tBinarySpaced().build();
3005
3006   /** Date type */
3007   public static final HttpPartSchema T_DATE = HttpPartSchema.tDate().build();
3008
3009   /** Date-time type */
3010   public static final HttpPartSchema T_DATETIME = HttpPartSchema.tDateTime().build();
3011
3012   /** UON-formated simple type */
3013   public static final HttpPartSchema T_UON = HttpPartSchema.tUon().build();
3014
3015   /** Array type */
3016   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY = HttpPartSchema.tArray().build();
3017
3018   /** Comma-delimited array type */
3019   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY_CSV = HttpPartSchema.tArrayCsv().build();
3020
3021   /** Pipe-delimited array type */
3022   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY_PIPES = HttpPartSchema.tArrayPipes().build();
3023
3024   /** Space-delimited array type */
3025   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY_SSV = HttpPartSchema.tArraySsv().build();
3026
3027   /** Tab-delimited array type */
3028   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY_TSV = HttpPartSchema.tArrayTsv().build();
3029
3030   /** UON-formatted array type */
3031   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY_UON = HttpPartSchema.tArrayUon().build();
3032
3033   /** Multi-part array type */
3034   public static final HttpPartSchema T_ARRAY_MULTI = HttpPartSchema.tArrayMulti().build();
3035
3036   /** Object type */
3037   public static final HttpPartSchema T_OBJECT = HttpPartSchema.tObject().build();
3038
3039   /** Comma-delimited object type */
3040   public static final HttpPartSchema T_OBJECT_CSV = HttpPartSchema.tObjectCsv().build();
3041
3042   /** Pipe-delimited object type */
3043   public static final HttpPartSchema T_OBJECT_PIPES = HttpPartSchema.tObjectPipes().build();
3044
3045   /** Space-delimited object type */
3046   public static final HttpPartSchema T_OBJECT_SSV = HttpPartSchema.tObjectSsv().build();
3047
3048   /** Tab-delimited object type */
3049   public static final HttpPartSchema T_OBJECT_TSV = HttpPartSchema.tObjectTsv().build();
3050
3051   /** UON-formated object type */
3052   public static final HttpPartSchema T_OBJECT_UON = HttpPartSchema.tObjectUon().build();
3053
3054   /**
3055    * Instantiates a new builder for this object.
3056    *
3057    * @return A new builder for this object.
3058    */
3059   public static Builder create() {
3060      return new Builder();
3061   }
3062
3063   /**
3064    * Finds the schema information on the specified annotation.
3065    *
3066    * @param a
3067    *    The annotation to find the schema information on..
3068    * @return The schema information found on the annotation.
3069    */
3070   public static HttpPartSchema create(Annotation a) {
3071      return create().apply(a).build();
3072   }
3073
3074   /**
3075    * Finds the schema information on the specified annotation.
3076    *
3077    * @param a
3078    *    The annotation to find the schema information on..
3079    * @param defaultName The default part name if not specified on the annotation.
3080    * @return The schema information found on the annotation.
3081    */
3082   public static HttpPartSchema create(Annotation a, String defaultName) {
3083      return create().name(defaultName).apply(a).build();
3084   }
3085
3086   /**
3087    * Finds the schema information for the specified class.
3088    *
3089    * <p>
3090    * This method will gather all the schema information from the annotations on the class and all parent classes/interfaces.
3091    *
3092    * @param c
3093    *    The annotation to look for.
3094    *    <br>Valid values:
3095    *    <ul>
3096    *       <li><ja>@Content</ja>
3097    *       <li><ja>@Header</ja>
3098    *       <li><ja>@Query</ja>
3099    *       <li><ja>@FormData</ja>
3100    *       <li><ja>@Path</ja>
3101    *       <li><ja>@Response</ja>
3102    *       <li><ja>@HasQuery</ja>
3103    *       <li><ja>@HasFormData</ja>
3104    *    </ul>
3105    * @param t
3106    *    The class containing the parameter.
3107    * @return The schema information about the parameter.
3108    */
3109   public static HttpPartSchema create(Class<? extends Annotation> c, Type t) {
3110      return create().applyAll(c, t).build();
3111   }
3112
3113   /**
3114    * Finds the schema information for the specified method return.
3115    *
3116    * <p>
3117    * This method will gather all the schema information from the annotations at the following locations:
3118    * <ul>
3119    *    <li>The method.
3120    *    <li>The method return class.
3121    *    <li>The method return parent classes and interfaces.
3122    * </ul>
3123    *
3124    * @param c
3125    *    The annotation to look for.
3126    *    <br>Valid values:
3127    *    <ul>
3128    *       <li><ja>@Content</ja>
3129    *       <li><ja>@Header</ja>
3130    *       <li><ja>@Query</ja>
3131    *       <li><ja>@FormData</ja>
3132    *       <li><ja>@Path</ja>
3133    *       <li><ja>@Response</ja>
3134    *       <li><ja>@HasQuery</ja>
3135    *       <li><ja>@HasFormData</ja>
3136    *    </ul>
3137    * @param m
3138    *    The Java method with the return type being checked.
3139    * @return The schema information about the parameter.
3140    */
3141   public static HttpPartSchema create(Class<? extends Annotation> c, Method m) {
3142      return create().applyAll(c, m).build();
3143   }
3144
3145   /**
3146    * Finds the schema information for the specified method parameter.
3147    *
3148    * <p>
3149    * This method will gather all the schema information from the annotations at the following locations:
3150    * <ul>
3151    *    <li>The method parameter.
3152    *    <li>The method parameter class.
3153    *    <li>The method parameter parent classes and interfaces.
3154    * </ul>
3155    *
3156    * @param c
3157    *    The annotation to look for.
3158    *    <br>Valid values:
3159    *    <ul>
3160    *       <li><ja>@Content</ja>
3161    *       <li><ja>@Header</ja>
3162    *       <li><ja>@Query</ja>
3163    *       <li><ja>@FormData</ja>
3164    *       <li><ja>@Path</ja>
3165    *       <li><ja>@Response</ja>
3166    *       <li><ja>@HasQuery</ja>
3167    *       <li><ja>@HasFormData</ja>
3168    *    </ul>
3169    * @param mpi The Java method parameter.
3170    * @return The schema information about the parameter.
3171    */
3172   public static HttpPartSchema create(Class<? extends Annotation> c, ParameterInfo mpi) {
3173      return create().applyAll(c, mpi).build();
3174   }
3175
3176   /**
3177    * Shortcut for calling <c>create().type(type);</c>
3178    *
3179    * @param type The schema type value.
3180    * @return A new builder.
3181    */
3182   public static Builder create(String type) {
3183      return create().type(type);
3184   }
3185
3186   /**
3187    * Shortcut for calling <c>create().type(type).format(format);</c>
3188    *
3189    * @param type The schema type value.
3190    * @param format The schema format value.
3191    * @return A new builder.
3192    */
3193   public static Builder create(String type, String format) {
3194      return create().type(type).format(format);
3195   }
3196
3197   /**
3198    * Shortcut for <c><jsm>create</jsm>().type(HttpPartDataType.<jsf>ARRAY</jsf>)</c>.
3199    *
3200    * @return A new builder for this object.
3201    */
3202   public static Builder tArray() {
3203      return create().tArray();
3204   }
3205
3206   /**
3207    * Shortcut for <c><jsm>create</jsm>().type(HttpPartDataType.<jsf>ARRAY</jsf>).items(items)</c>.
3208    *
3209    * @param items The schema of the array items.
3210    * @return A new builder for this object.
3211    */
3212   public static Builder tArray(Builder items) {
3213      return create().tArray().items(items);
3214   }
3215
3216   /**
3217    * Shortcut for <c><jsm>create</jsm>().type(HttpPartDataType.<jsf>ARRAY</jsf>).collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.<jsf>CSV</jsf>)</c>.
3218    *
3219    * @return A new builder for this object.
3220    */
3221   public static Builder tArrayCsv() {
3222      return create().tArray().cfCsv();
3223   }
3224
3225   /**
3226    * Shortcut for <c><jsm>create</jsm>().type(HttpPartDataType.<jsf>ARRAY</jsf>).collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat.<jsf>CSV</jsf>).items(items)</c>.
3227    *
3228    * @param items The schema of the array items.
3229    * @return A new builder for this object.
3230    */
3231   public static Builder tArrayCsv(Builder items) {
3232      return create().tArray().cfCsv().items(items);
3233   }
